name: Flag France
desc: The national flag of France with blue, white, and red vertical stripes.

name: Croissant
desc: A flaky, buttery French pastry shaped like a crescent moon.

name: Baguette Bread
desc: A long, thin loaf of French bread with a crispy crust.

name: Cheese Wedge
desc: A wedge of yellow cheese, representing the many French cheese varieties.

name: Wine Glass
desc: A glass of red wine, representing famous French wine regions like Bordeaux.

name: Clinking Glasses
desc: Champagne glasses toasting, celebrating French sparkling wine.

name: Bottle with Popping Cork
desc: A champagne bottle with a popping cork, iconic French celebration drink.

name: Castle
desc: A medieval French castle like those found in the Loire Valley.

name: Church
desc: A church representing famous French cathedrals like Notre-Dame de Paris.

name: Eiffel Tower
desc: The iconic Eiffel Tower, the most famous landmark in Paris, France.

name: Classical Building
desc: Classical French architecture representing the Louvre Museum.

name: Artist Palette
desc: Representing French art, the Louvre, and the Impressionist movement.

name: Kiss Mark
desc: The French kiss mark, representing French romance and affection.

name: Red Heart
desc: The city of love - Paris is known as the most romantic city in the world.

name: Cherry Blossom
desc: Beautiful spring flowers in French gardens like the Luxembourg Garden.

name: Rose
desc: The rose, a symbol of beauty and the French art of living.

name: Hot Beverage
desc: French café culture and espresso enjoyed at Parisian sidewalk cafes.

name: French Pastry
desc: Delicious French pastries from a traditional patisserie shop.

name: Shopping Bags
desc: Shopping at boutiques along the famous Champs-Élysées in Paris.

name: Bicycle
desc: Cycling in France, home of the famous Tour de France race.

name: Fleur-de-lis
desc: The fleur-de-lis, a traditional French royal symbol and emblem.

name: Flag European Union
desc: The European Union flag, France is a founding member of the EU.

name: Cook
desc: A French chef representing world-famous French haute cuisine.

name: Green Salad
desc: A fresh Niçoise salad, a classic dish from the South of France.

name: Snail
desc: Escargot, a classic French delicacy of cooked land snails.

name: Rooster
desc: The rooster (coq), a national symbol of France.

name: Beach with Umbrella
desc: The French Riviera beaches, including Nice and Saint-Tropez.

name: Water Wave
desc: The Mediterranean Sea along the southern coast of France.
